top_findings: robots.txt blocks ALL crawlers (Disallow: /) — site is completely invisible to Google and every AI engine
Global noindex/nofollow in layout.tsx — second independent layer preventing indexation
Blog post pages (/blog/[slug]) do not exist — 6 internal links lead to 404s
OG image /og-image.jpg missing from /public — social sharing previews are broken
Indian phone number (+91) on a US-targeted site — critical trust and entity signal conflict
quick_wins: Fix robots.txt: replace 'Disallow: /' with 'Allow: /' — 30 minutes, unlocks all crawling
Remove robots: { index: false } from layout.tsx — 5 minutes, removes global noindex
Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD to the existing Breadcrumb component — breadcrumb data already exists
Create /public/llms.txt — 2 hours, enables AI crawler guidance
Add AggregateRating schema to root JSON-LD — testimonial data already in page.tsx

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: Sitemap.xml exists with 11 URLs, correct lastmod and priority values
Canonical tags set on all inner pages via Next.js metadata alternates.canonical
lang="en" on html element
next/font/google — zero render-blocking font requests
next/image — automatic WebP/AVIF, lazy loading, size optimization

findings.title: robots.txt blocks all crawlers
findings.description: Disallow: / under User-Agent: * prevents Googlebot, Bingbot, GPTBot, ClaudeBot, and every other crawler from accessing any page on the site.
findings.recommendation: Replace with: User-agent: * / Allow: / / Sitemap: https://pinexadigital.com/sitemap.xml — remove the non-standard Host: directive.

findings.title: Global meta noindex/nofollow in layout.tsx
findings.description: layout.tsx sets robots: { index: false, follow: false } — emits noindex, nofollow on every page. Second independent blocking layer even if robots.txt is fixed.
findings.recommendation: Change to robots: { index: true, follow: true } in layout.tsx. Apply noindex at page level only where genuinely needed.

findings.title: Homepage is a Client Component with no metadata export
findings.description: app/page.tsx uses 'use client' and exports no metadata. Next.js App Router cannot export metadata from Client Components, so the homepage inherits only layout defaults including the global noindex.
findings.recommendation: Split into a Server Component (page.tsx, exports metadata) wrapping a Client Component (HomeClient.tsx) for interactive UI.

findings.title: OG image /og-image.jpg missing from /public
findings.description: All metadata (OG and Twitter card) references /og-image.jpg which does not exist. Social sharing previews will show no image.
findings.recommendation: Create a 1200×630px branded JPEG at /public/og-image.jpg.

findings.title: 6 blog post pages return 404
findings.description: All 6 blog post links in /blog lead to non-existent /blog/[slug] routes. Creates crawl errors and wastes PageRank flow.
findings.recommendation: Build /src/app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx dynamic route with real content, or remove blog links and update sitemap until pages exist.

findings.title: Contact form POSTs to /api/contact which does not exist
findings.description: ContactForm.tsx submits to /api/contact but no Route Handler exists. Every submission silently fails — business-critical functional failure.
findings.recommendation: Create app/api/contact/route.ts with POST handler. Use Resend or Nodemailer for email delivery.

findings.title: No security headers in next.config.ts
findings.description: Missing CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy. Security scanners will grade F.
findings.recommendation: Add a headers() export to next.config.ts covering all standard security headers.

findings.title: Sitemap lists URLs that return 404
findings.description: Sitemap.xml should only list canonically indexable, 200-status pages. After fixing blog routes, regenerate sitemap dynamically.
findings.recommendation: Use app/sitemap.ts with a dynamic export. Resubmit to Google Search Console after fixing.

findings.title: No llms.txt file
findings.description: AI crawlers (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot) have no structured guidance file.
findings.recommendation: Create /public/llms.txt with site name, description, key URL list, and AI usage declarations.

findings.title: Non-standard Host: directive in robots.txt
findings.description: Host: directive is Yandex-only, ignored by Google and Bing. Adds noise.
findings.recommendation: Remove the Host: line from robots.txt.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Transparent fixed pricing published openly ($997/$2,497/Custom)
Clear site architecture with breadcrumbs on all inner pages
Service sub-pages have numbered process steps and feature inclusion lists
FAQ section on pricing page is structurally ready for schema markup
Page-level metadata (title, description, OG, Twitter card) correctly implemented on inner pages

findings.title: All 6 blog posts are 404 — blog is completely broken
findings.description: Blog posts dated March–June 2025 link to routes that return 404. No /app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx exists. Most recent post is 13 months old as of June 2026.
findings.recommendation: Build the dynamic route and write 1,500+ word articles for at least 2-3 posts. Add author names and a 2026-dated post immediately.

findings.title: Indian phone number (+91) on a US-targeted site
findings.description: +91 78198 32001 appears on the contact page and in JSON-LD schema. Contradicts the US-specialist positioning and causes immediate trust failure with US prospects.
findings.recommendation: Obtain a US virtual number (Google Voice, Grasshopper, OpenPhone ~$10-25/month) and update site-config.ts and schema.

findings.title: About page has no named humans
findings.description: No founder name, no team names, no photos, no credentials. Generic origin story matches common AI-generated agency template patterns. US clients cannot determine who they are paying.
findings.recommendation: Add founder/team section with real full names, 2-3 sentence bios, headshots, and LinkedIn links.

findings.title: Services overview page is critically thin (~285 words vs 800-word minimum)
findings.description: Four service cards with ~30-50 words each. Less than 35% of the topical minimum for a commercial service page.
findings.recommendation: Expand each service section to 200+ words with problem/solution framing, differentiators, and a mini-FAQ. Target 1,000+ total words.

findings.title: Testimonials are completely unverifiable — initials only, no photos or links
findings.description: Sarah M., James R., Diana L. — initials-only avatars, no headshots, no company URLs, no review platform links. A 4th avatar (TK) has no corresponding testimonial.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to full name + company name linked to their website. Add Google Business Profile or Clutch review links.

findings.title: Homepage stats are unverified marketing claims
findings.description: 50+ projects, 98% satisfaction, 21 days delivery, 100% 5-star reviews — no third-party verification. Hero dashboard shows fictional +127% growth figures.
findings.recommendation: Add source attribution per stat. Link 5-star claim to a real review platform. Replace fictional dashboard with real client screenshot.

findings.title: Blog last updated 13 months ago
findings.description: Most recent post: June 1, 2025. Current date: June 29, 2026. Signals abandoned blog to Google freshness systems.
findings.recommendation: Publish at least one substantive 2026 post before enabling indexing. Aim for monthly cadence.

findings.title: No Privacy Policy or Terms of Service
findings.description: Contact form collects user data. No Privacy Policy, no cookie notice, no Terms of Service. CCPA/CAN-SPAM compliance gap and quality rater trust flag.
findings.recommendation: Add /privacy-policy and /terms pages. Link from footer. Generate from a reputable privacy policy generator.

findings.title: No author bylines on blog listing
findings.description: No author name attributed to any blog post. Combined with anonymous About page, no human identity is attached to any content.
findings.recommendation: Add author field to post data. Create author bio pages linked from each post.

findings.title: No Google Business Profile or third-party review profile linked
findings.description: '100% 5-star reviews' claim with no verification path anywhere.
findings.recommendation: Create and verify a Google Business Profile. Also consider Clutch.co (standard in US web agency market).

findings.title: Blog titles reference '2025' — stale by 12+ months
findings.description: Two post titles include '2025' — will appear outdated to visitors arriving in 2026.
findings.recommendation: Update to '2026' equivalents or use evergreen titles with a 'Last updated' byline when content is published.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Title template system working: '%s | PinexaDigital' on inner pages
Meta descriptions present and distinct on all inner pages
H1 tags are keyword-relevant and distinct across all pages (no duplication)
Canonical tags set on all inner pages
Breadcrumb navigation present on all inner pages
Keywords array set in root metadata (harmless for Google, may help Bing)

findings.title: Homepage title is brand-first with no keyword
findings.description: Homepage inherits the layout default: 'PinexaDigital — Web Design & Development Agency'. 'PinexaDigital' is an unknown brand for new US visitors. A keyword-first title would improve CTR.
findings.recommendation: Once the homepage has a Server Component wrapper with metadata export, set title: 'Web Design Agency for US Businesses | PinexaDigital'.

findings.title: No keyword targeting on any page beyond broad terms
findings.description: Service pages target very broad terms ('web design', 'SEO'). No city-level or vertical-specific targeting (e.g. 'web design for US restaurants' or 'web design Austin TX').
findings.recommendation: After indexing is fixed, run keyword research for commercial intent US queries. Consider adding location or vertical landing pages for highest-converting segments.

findings.title: Services overview page has no unique keyword content
findings.description: The /services page is largely a category page with thin descriptions that repeat what sub-pages say. No unique angle or content.
findings.recommendation: Give the services overview a distinct purpose: comparison table, buyer's guide framing, or 'which service do I need' decision tree.

findings.title: Internal linking is shallow — blog and portfolio link everywhere but nothing links back to them
findings.description: Navigation links exist but deep content pages (blog posts, portfolio cases) are not referenced from service pages as supporting evidence.
findings.recommendation: Add contextual internal links from service pages to relevant blog posts and portfolio case studies once they exist.

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: ProfessionalService @type is semantically appropriate
@context correctly uses https
Core identity fields present: name, url, description, telephone, email, priceRange
JSON-LD format used (correct over Microdata or RDFa)
siteUrl() helper centralises the domain

findings.title: Global noindex makes all schema inert
findings.description: Google does not show rich results for pages it cannot index. Every schema improvement below is moot until indexing is enabled.
findings.recommendation: Fix the noindex issue first (see Technical SEO findings). Then validate schema via Google Rich Results Test.

findings.title: No BreadcrumbList schema despite a Breadcrumb component on every inner page
findings.description: The Breadcrumb component renders correctly but emits zero structured data. BreadcrumbList is one of Google's most reliably triggered rich results.
findings.recommendation: Inject JSON-LD BreadcrumbList inside the Breadcrumb component, built from the same crumbs prop already available.

findings.title: No Service schema on any of the four service pages
findings.description: Each service page describes a distinct offering with name, description, deliverables, and pricing — but no Service schema.
findings.recommendation: Add @type: Service JSON-LD to each service page with name, description, provider, areaServed, and offers with PriceSpecification.

findings.title: No PriceSpecification or Offer schema on pricing page
findings.description: Three tiers ($997, $2,497, Custom) with full feature lists — no structured data. Google and LLMs must infer pricing from raw text.
findings.recommendation: Add an ItemList or individual Offer blocks for the two fixed tiers. Use InvoicePrice for the custom tier.

findings.title: No AggregateRating schema despite explicit 5-star claims
findings.description: Homepage shows '5.0 Stars' badge and five filled stars on 3 testimonials but no AggregateRating schema. Lost star snippet in SERPs.
findings.recommendation: Add AggregateRating to the ProfessionalService block: ratingValue 5.0, reviewCount 3, plus individual Review entities for each testimonial.

findings.title: areaServed includes 'IN' (India) contradicting US-only positioning
findings.description: areaServed: ['US', 'IN'] sends a conflicting signal when all site copy targets US businesses exclusively.
findings.recommendation: Change to areaServed: { '@type': 'Country', 'name': 'United States' }.

findings.title: No logo property on root ProfessionalService block
findings.description: Google's Knowledge Panel uses the logo property. Without it, Google must guess, leading to inconsistent display.
findings.recommendation: Add logo: { '@type': 'ImageObject', 'url': 'https://pinexadigital.com/logo.png', 'width': 512, 'height': 512 }.

findings.title: No sameAs links to social profiles
findings.description: sameAs is the primary mechanism for Google's Knowledge Graph to connect the entity to external identity signals.
findings.recommendation: Add sameAs array with LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Clutch, and Google Business Profile URLs once created.

findings.title: No WebSite schema with SearchAction
findings.description: Missing WebSite schema for Knowledge Panel brand entity association.
findings.recommendation: Add @type: WebSite block alongside ProfessionalService using @graph to group both.

findings.title: Blog listing has no Blog or ItemList schema; blog post pages don't exist yet
findings.description: Blog listing renders 6 post cards with titles, excerpts, dates — all data needed for ItemList. No structured data emitted.
findings.recommendation: Add ItemList to blog listing now. Add BlogPosting schema per post when routes are built.

findings.title: FAQPage schema absent from pricing page — Info level, SERP value is zero
findings.description: FAQPage schema lost Google SERP rich result support on May 7, 2026. No SERP benefit. Still useful for AI/GEO citation if that is a priority.
findings.recommendation: Optional: add FAQPage for AI citation benefit only. Do not expect SERP rich results.

categories.name: Performance (CWV)
what_works: next/image for automatic WebP/AVIF conversion and lazy loading
next/font/google — zero render-blocking requests, font-display: swap
Next.js App Router SSR for inner pages (not homepage)
Minimal third-party scripts (no tag managers, chat widgets, or ad pixels detected)

findings.title: Hero image likely missing priority prop — LCP risk
findings.description: next/image lazy-loads by default. If the above-the-fold hero image has no priority prop, LCP will be delayed. The main Unsplash image in the Why Us section uses priority but is below the fold.
findings.recommendation: Add priority prop to the hero section's largest above-the-fold image. Verify LCP after fix using PageSpeed Insights.

findings.title: Homepage fully Client-Side Rendered — INP risk on mobile
findings.description: Entire homepage is a Client Component with Framer Motion animations. Heavy hydration on mid-range Android devices can push INP above 200ms threshold.
findings.recommendation: Split homepage into Server + Client Components. Use dynamic imports with { ssr: false } for heavy animation components. Run next build and check route chunk sizes.

findings.title: External Unsplash images — CDN dependency and stock photo trust issue
findings.description: Homepage and about page use Unsplash stock images. Next.js caches them but CDN performance depends on Unsplash uptime. Stock photos also reduce perceived authenticity.
findings.recommendation: Replace with genuine agency/team photos for E-E-A-T benefit. If stock is kept, download and self-host in /public to eliminate the external CDN dependency.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness
what_works: Pricing page has structured, specific data — most citable page on the site
Process enumerated in numbered steps — AI-extractable format
Blog post titles target high-intent question queries — topic selection is correct
ProfessionalService JSON-LD present site-wide

findings.title: robots.txt blocks all AI crawlers — site invisible to GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot
findings.description: Disallow: / blocks GPTBot, OAI-SearchBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended. Site cannot be cited by any AI search engine.
findings.recommendation: Fix robots.txt first. This single change unlocks all AI crawler access.

findings.title: No llms.txt — AI agents cannot discover site structure
findings.description: llms.txt is absent. AI agents have no machine-readable declaration of content availability or permissions.
findings.recommendation: Create /public/llms.txt with site name, description, key URL list, and AI usage declarations.

findings.title: Blog posts return 404 — highest-citability content type is broken
findings.description: Blog post titles match high-volume AI-answerable queries but every URL 404s.
findings.recommendation: Publish blog posts with 600-800+ words, author names, cited statistics, and self-contained answer blocks in each H2.

findings.title: Zero named authorship — AI citation systems deprioritise anonymous content
findings.description: No page attributes content to a named human. AI citation systems apply E-E-A-T signals; anonymous content is systematically deprioritized.
findings.recommendation: Add named founder/author with bio and LinkedIn. Add Person schema linked from Article schema on blog posts.

findings.title: Content passages too short for AI citation (40-90 words vs 134-167 word optimum)
findings.description: Every section on the site falls below the optimal passage length for AI extraction. No self-contained answer blocks exist.
findings.recommendation: Expand service cards and process steps to 150+ words. Open each section with a direct answer sentence.

findings.title: +91 phone number creates entity classification as Indian business
findings.description: AI systems use contact geography as a trust signal for local relevance. The Indian number undermines US entity classification.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a US virtual number and update schema.

findings.title: No brand signals on YouTube, Reddit, LinkedIn, or Clutch
findings.description: YouTube mentions correlate at ~0.737 with AI citation frequency. Zero off-site brand presence means near-zero AI citation probability.
findings.recommendation: Priority: YouTube channel (3-5 Q&A videos), then Reddit participation, then Clutch.co profile with verified reviews.

findings.title: No FAQ schema or question-based headings
findings.description: All headings are statements ('Our Services', 'Why Choose Us'). AI Overviews preferentially surface question-structured content.
findings.recommendation: Add FAQ sections to homepage, pricing, and each service page. Use question-format headings and FAQPage schema.

categories.name: Images
what_works: next/image used for all images — automatic optimization, lazy loading
Alt text present on visible images ('PinexaDigital team at work', 'Design process')
priority prop used on main hero image in Why Us section

findings.title: OG image /og-image.jpg missing
findings.description: Referenced in OG and Twitter card metadata but file does not exist in /public. All social sharing will show no image.
findings.recommendation: Create 1200×630px branded JPEG at /public/og-image.jpg.

findings.title: Only 2 images on homepage — image-light for SEO and engagement
findings.description: Homepage has one team photo and one process thumbnail. Very image-sparse for a design agency whose primary product is visual.
findings.recommendation: Add portfolio screenshots, before/after website examples, or team photos. Each with descriptive alt text targeting relevant keywords.

findings.title: Stock photos from Unsplash reduce authenticity signals
findings.description: Unsplash stock images are recognizable to repeat visitors and quality raters. Design agencies are expected to showcase real work.
findings.recommendation: Replace with genuine photos of the workspace, team, or client project screenshots.

phases.name: Phase 1: Unblock the Site
items: Fix robots.txt — replace Disallow: / with Allow: /
Remove robots: { index: false } from layout.tsx
Obtain US virtual phone number and update site-config.ts + schema
Create /public/og-image.jpg (1200×630px branded card)
Add founder/team name and bio to About page
Add Privacy Policy (/privacy-policy) and Terms of Service (/terms) pages
Create /api/contact route handler (contact form is currently broken)

phases.name: Phase 2: Fix Critical Content Gaps
items: Build /src/app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx dynamic route
Write 1,500+ word content for top 3 blog posts with author name and BlogPosting schema
Publish one 2026-dated blog post to reset freshness signal
Split homepage into Server Component (page.tsx) + Client Component (HomeClient.tsx) to enable metadata export
Upgrade testimonials to full name + company link (or link to Google Business Profile)

phases.name: Phase 3: Schema and On-Page Improvements
items: Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD to Breadcrumb component
Add AggregateRating + Review schema to root JSON-LD
Fix areaServed: remove 'IN', use { '@type': 'Country', 'name': 'United States' }
Add Service + Offer schema to each service page
Add PriceSpecification / ItemList schema to pricing page
Add WebSite @type with @id to layout JSON-LD
Add logo property to ProfessionalService schema
Add security headers to next.config.ts
Create /public/llms.txt
Expand services overview page to 1,000+ words
Update sitemap.xml to use dynamic app/sitemap.ts

phases.name: Phase 4: Authority and AI Visibility
items: Create and verify Google Business Profile — collect first 5 real reviews
Build Clutch.co profile (verified B2B review platform, AI systems cite it)
Launch YouTube channel with 3-5 short videos targeting blog post queries
Add sameAs to schema linking to GBP, LinkedIn, Clutch, YouTube
Add portfolio case studies with real client URLs, screenshots, and measurable outcomes
Participate in r/smallbusiness and r/entrepreneur — answer questions authentically
Expand all service sub-pages to 800-1,200 words with FAQ sections
Add FAQ schema to homepage and each service page
Set up Google Search Console and submit corrected sitemap
